title: Identity Primitive
description: Immutable identifiers and canonical URIs for governed objects. Constitutional properties: dual identity (id/uri), orthogonal versioning, immutability, lineage tracking, and canonical URI template conformance.

id.description: Stable logical identifier for internal references. Does not require full URI resolution.

uri.description: Canonical globally unique identifier conforming to RFC 3986. Resolves to the authoritative representation of the governed object.

version.description: Semantic version (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) of the identified object. Version is orthogonal to identity — changing version does not change identity.

immutable.description: Constitutional invariant. Once published, identity can never change. Identity mutation requires a new artifact, new URI, and new lineage.

lineage.description: Provenance and succession history. Enables governance traceability without polluting identity.

parent.description: URI of the parent object from which this identity was derived. null for root objects.

supersedes.description: URIs of objects that this identity replaces.

superseded_by.description: URI of the object that supersedes this identity. null if active.
